phases.name: Close the absent-workload GC gap in WorkloadLifecycle

steps.name: Add StoppedBy::SystemGC variant + schema-evolution fixture pin
steps.criteria: Per ADR-0037 Amendment 2026-05-14: extend `StoppedBy` in `crates/overdrive-core/src/transition_reason.rs` with a new `SystemGC` variant appended at discriminant index 3 (after `Process=2`). The variant addition lands ALONGSIDE a comment-update on `Process` in the SAME commit: the existing 'MUST remain the last variant to preserve rkyv discriminant compatibility' wording (lines 212-213) is structurally stale once `SystemGC` is appended â€” the invariant is discriminant stability for pre-existing variants, not 'Process must be last'. Replace the stale wording with the canonical phrasing used at `TerminalCondition::Completed` (~lines 395-401 of the same file): 'Appended after `Process` to keep the pre-existing rkyv discriminants stable. This variant takes discriminant `3`. Existing archived rows decode unchanged.' Landing the variant without the comment fix leaves a contradicted invariant claim in the SSOT and is rejected at review. Variant is `#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]`-compatible (serialises as `"system_gc"`). The crate compiles workspace-wide: every exhaustive `match` / `matches!` on `StoppedBy` in `crates/overdrive-core/src/`, `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/`, `crates/overdrive-worker/src/` (and the broader workspace) gains a `SystemGC` arm â€” closed-set switches without `#[non_exhaustive]` produce compile errors the step must resolve. Schema-evolution coverage per `.claude/rules/development.md` Â§ 'rkyv schema evolution' â†’ 'Version-bump procedure': existing `FIXTURE_V1` at `crates/overdrive-core/tests/schema_evolution/alloc_status_row.rs:59` MUST NOT be touched. Adding a `StoppedBy` variant does NOT bump the envelope to V2 â€” the rkyv layout is unchanged; only an enum gained an additive variant at the tail. The existing `FIXTURE_V1` decode-and-project test continues to pass unchanged and serves as the regression guard against accidental envelope drift. Add a NEW forward-roundtrip test (NOT a fixture constant) to the same file: construct a fresh `AllocStatusRow` with `terminal = Some(TerminalCondition::Stopped { by: StoppedBy::SystemGC })` via the canonical builder, rkyv-archive through the current `AllocStatusRowEnvelope`, rkyv-deserialise, assert `Eq` against the source. This proves the new variant encodes/decodes through the existing envelope; no `FIXTURE_V2` minted. The variant is dead code at the end of this step (no production emitter exists yet â€” wired in step 01-02); the forward roundtrip test exercises it, so `dead_code` is unlikely to fire, but a step-scoped `#[expect(dead_code, reason = "emitter lands in step 01-02")]` is permitted if needed. Mutation-killability: a mutant swapping `StoppedBy::SystemGC` for `StoppedBy::Process` in the forward-roundtrip test must fail; a mutant changing the discriminant number in the source (renumbering `Process` from 2) breaks `FIXTURE_V1` decode (regression caught). Closes structural prerequisite for #148 AC Â§1.3 'distinct TerminalCondition'.

steps.name: WorkloadLifecycle reconcile None-arm emits StopAllocation { terminal: SystemGC } per orphan Running row
steps.criteria: Replace the current no-op `None => (Vec::new(), view.clone())` arm at `crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs:1206-1211` with the GC body specified by `docs/feature/workload-gc-absent-stale-allocs/design/architecture.md` Â§ 4 Option A. Structural mirror of the operator-stop branch immediately above (`reconciler.rs:1180-1205`): iterate `actual.allocations.values().filter(|r| r.state == AllocState::Running)`, emit one `Action::StopAllocation { alloc_id: r.alloc_id.clone(), terminal: Some(TerminalCondition::Stopped { by: StoppedBy::SystemGC }) }` per row, return `(stop_actions, next_view)` where `next_view = view.clone()` with `last_failure_seen_at.clear()` applied iff `stop_actions.is_empty()` (matches the Stop branch's view-cleanup shape â€” input clearance, not derived-deadline clearance, per `.claude/rules/development.md` Â§ 'Persist inputs, not derived state'). The `TODO(#148)` comment is removed; the new comment cites issue #148 and ADR-0037 Amendment 2026-05-14. Unit tests in `crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/workload_lifecycle_reconcile_branches.rs` (port-to-port through `WorkloadLifecycle::reconcile` â€” the pure-function driving port): (a) `desired.job == None` AND `actual.allocations` contains N>=1 `Running` rows produces N `Action::StopAllocation` whose `terminal == Some(TerminalCondition::Stopped { by: StoppedBy::SystemGC })`, one per row, indexed by `alloc_id` (assert on the multiset, not list order); (b) `desired.job == None` AND `actual.allocations` is empty produces zero actions AND `next_view.last_failure_seen_at` is cleared; (c) `desired.job == None` AND every row already terminal (`Terminated`/`Failed`/`Completed`) produces zero actions AND `next_view.last_failure_seen_at` is cleared (idempotency / steady-state); (d) mixed: orphan workload with two `Running` rows and one `Terminated` row emits exactly two `StopAllocation` actions for the Running rows, none for the terminal row. Tests assert observable outcomes (actions emitted, view state) through the driving port â€” no internal-state introspection. Workload-kind-agnostic: the four tests parametrise over `WorkloadKind` âˆˆ {Service, Job, Schedule} to confirm the arm body branches on `desired.job.is_none()` not on kind (per architecture.md Â§ 8 Open Question 2: 'not in scope today' to vary by kind). Filter-shape decision (resolves architecture.md Â§ 8 Open Question 3 in-scope): the GC arm filters `state == Running`, mirroring the operator-Stop branch exactly. A `Pending` row has no driver-side runtime to stop, and a `Draining` row is already being torn down by the worker â€” neither requires a fresh `StopAllocation` Action. Test (d) explicitly covers the mixed-states case: an orphan workload with `[Pending, Running, Draining, Terminated]` rows produces exactly ONE `StopAllocation` (for the Running row), zero for the others. If the crafter finds during implementation that a Pending or Draining row can be left stranded with no other observer advancing it, that is a real defect â€” surface to the user as a blocker (with the specific reproduction shape); do NOT silently widen the filter without user approval per CLAUDE.md Â§ 'Deferrals require GitHub issues â€” AND user approval BEFORE creation'. Mutation-killability targets: a mutant flipping `Running` to `Terminated` in the filter must fail test (a); a mutant flipping `is_empty()` to `is_empty().not()` in the view-cleanup branch must fail tests (b)+(c); a mutant replacing `StoppedBy::SystemGC` with `StoppedBy::Operator` must fail test (a). Closes #148 AC Â§1.3 'convergence' and 'distinct TerminalCondition' at the reconciler-pure-function boundary.

steps.name: DST scenarios for orphan convergence and resubmit race
steps.criteria: Land the two Tier 1 DST scenarios from `docs/feature/workload-gc-absent-stale-allocs/design/architecture.md` Â§ 7, driving turmoil through the existing `SimIntentStore` + `SimObservationStore` + WorkloadLifecycle runtime stack. Both scenarios drive end-to-end convergence â€” entry through the public `submit` / `tick` harness driving ports, assertions on `ObservationStore::alloc_status_rows()` (a driven port boundary). No reconciler internals exercised directly. Scenario 1 â€” `orphan_workload_converges_to_terminal_gc`: (setup) submit `Job(id=X)`; wait until at least one alloc row reaches `Running`; (fault) call `IntentStore::delete(b"jobs/X")` on the harness's intent handle â€” the primitive already exists in the trait at `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/intent_store.rs:193` (`async fn delete(&self, key: &[u8]) -> Result<(), IntentStoreError>`); no new wiring required. Also confirm at this step that the action shim sequences `AllocStatusRow.terminal` durability before lifecycle-event broadcast for `Action::StopAllocation` (per architecture.md Â§ 4 Option A 'Within-shim ordering' crafter-validation gate); if the shim broadcasts before durability, surface as a blocker to the user (do NOT silently absorb a within-shim race). (drive) advance ticks until quiescence under `assert_eventually!`; three invariants â€” (gc.converges) every `alloc_status_rows().filter(|r| r.workload_id == X)` row eventually has `r.state.is_terminal() == true`; (gc.terminal_claim) the same filtered rows all carry `r.terminal == Some(TerminalCondition::Stopped { by: StoppedBy::SystemGC })`; (gc.no_fresh_alloc) `assert_always!` â€” no alloc row whose `workload_id == X` is created after the fault-injection logical timestamp while intent stays absent (count delta over the post-fault tick window == 0). Scenario 2 â€” `resubmit_after_gc_creates_fresh_alloc`: continues from Scenario 1 quiescent state; (action) resubmit `Job(id=X)` to intent; (drive) advance ticks; two invariants â€” (resubmit.places_fresh) `assert_eventually!` â‰¥1 alloc row with `workload_id == X` reaches `Running` AND that alloc's `alloc_id != original_alloc_id` (durable distinctness â€” the GC'd row is not resurrected); (resubmit.preserves_prior_gc_terminal) `assert_always!` the original alloc row's `terminal` stays `Some(TerminalCondition::Stopped { by: StoppedBy::SystemGC })` for every tick after resubmit â€” the SystemGC stamp is durable through the resubmit cycle. Each `assert_eventually!` carries an explicit `max_ticks` bound (3 for gc.converges/gc.terminal_claim; 5 for resubmit.places_fresh) per architecture.md Â§ 7 â€” an unbounded 'eventually' is satisfied by buggy implementations that converge through unrelated paths; the budget is the falsifiability gate. If the existing harness's `assert_eventually!` macro does not yet accept a `max_ticks` parameter, wire the budget at the harness level in this step (additive macro extension; existing scenarios that rely on a default budget continue working). Both scenarios run under deterministic seeds; on failure the seed prints per `.claude/rules/testing.md` Â§ 'Seeding and reproducibility'. Scenarios live under `crates/overdrive-sim/tests/integration/` (the existing Tier 1 DST harness location) as `workload_gc_absent_intent.rs`, declared from `tests/integration.rs`. Mutation-killability targets at the reconciler-pure-function call site (which the DST drives transitively): a mutant flipping the `SystemGC` stamp to `Operator` must fail (gc.terminal_claim) AND (resubmit.preserves_prior_gc_terminal); a mutant skipping the StopAllocation emission entirely (returning `Vec::new()`) must fail (gc.converges); a mutant turning the `actual.allocations` filter into `.collect_vec()` without the `Running` predicate must produce stray actions that violate the existing reconciler-purity / no-duplicate-stop invariants. Closes #148 AC Â§1.3 'DST scenario covering the absent-workload-stale-running shape'. The full per-PR mutation gate (`cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-core`) runs after this step and gates merge per `.claude/rules/testing.md` Â§ 'Mutation testing (cargo-mutants)' â€” diff-scoped, kill-rate â‰¥ 80%.
